name: CRUD/delete
description: Conformance tests for instance delete (DELETE) semantics ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E Rest/DeleteTests.cs: a deleted resource returns 410 Gone (or 404) on subsequent read while its pre-delete version remains vread-able, deleted resources are excluded from subsequent searches, hard delete permanently erases all version history (extended capability, warningOnly), and the $purge-history operation removes prior versions while keeping the current version accessible (extended capability, warningOnly). Fixtures are tagged identifier DELETE-* (system http://ignixa.io/testscript/suite/test) to avoid colliding with other suites on a shared test server.

variable.name: deleteBasicId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the basic delete-lifecycle Patient

variable.name: deleteBasicVersion
variable.description: server-assigned initial versionId of the basic delete-lifecycle Patient, expected to remain vread-able after delete

variable.name: deleteSearchId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the deleted-excluded-from-search test

variable.name: hardDeleteId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the hard-delete test

variable.name: hardDeleteVersion
variable.description: initial versionId of the hard-delete Patient, used to confirm it is no longer vread-able after a hard delete

variable.name: purgeId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the $purge-history test

variable.name: purgeVersion1
variable.description: first versionId of the purge-history Patient, expected to be purged

variable.name: purgeVersion2
variable.description: second (current) versionId of the purge-history Patient, expected to remain accessible after $purge-history

test.name: delete removes the resource; a plain read is Gone/NotFound while the original version remains vread-able
test.description: DELETE Patient/{id} must succeed. A subsequent plain read must return 410 Gone (or 404 Not Found as a warningOnly alternative), while a vread of the version that existed before the delete must still succeed.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will be deleted

assert.description: Create must return HTTP 201

operation.description: Delete the Patient

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Attempt to read the deleted Patient

assert.description: Primary: expect 410 Gone
Alternative: 404 Not Found is also accepted

operation.description: vread the pre-delete version of the Patient

assert.description: vread of the pre-delete version must still return HTTP 200
Response body must be a Patient
Returned id must match the deleted Patient's id

test.name: deleted resource does not appear in subsequent searches
test.description: A Patient tagged with a unique identifier is created, confirmed searchable, deleted, and then confirmed absent from the same identifier search.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will be searched for, then deleted

assert.description: Create must return HTTP 201

operation.description: Search by identifier before delete; the Patient must be present

assert.description: Search must return HTTP 200
Response must be a Bundle
Must include the created Patient before delete

operation.description: Delete the Patient

assert.description: Delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Search by the same identifier after delete; the Patient must be excluded

assert.description: Search must return HTTP 200
Response must be a Bundle
Must NOT include the deleted Patient after delete

test.name: hard delete permanently removes the resource and all version history
test.description: Extended capability (not mandated by base FHIR R4): DELETE Patient/{id}?hardDelete=true after a normal (soft) delete must permanently erase the resource so that a plain read, a vread of its original version, and its instance history all return 404 Not Found. All assertions are warningOnly since hard delete is a server extension not every conformant server implements.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will be soft- then hard-deleted

assert.description: Create must return HTTP 201

operation.description: Soft-delete the Patient first

assert.description: Soft delete must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Hard-delete the Patient (server extension parameter)

assert.description: Server extension: expect a 2xx success status when hard delete is supported

operation.description: Attempt to read the hard-deleted Patient

assert.description: Server extension: expect 404 Not Found for a hard-deleted resource

operation.description: Attempt to vread the hard-deleted Patient's original version

assert.description: Server extension: expect 404 Not Found for a purged version

operation.description: Attempt to fetch instance history for the hard-deleted Patient

assert.description: Server extension: expect 404 Not Found for the history of a hard-deleted resource

test.name: $purge-history removes prior versions while keeping the current version accessible
test.description: Extended capability (not mandated by base FHIR R4): after updating a resource so it has two versions, POST/DELETE Patient/{id}/$purge-history must remove the prior version(s) while the current version stays fully readable. All assertions are warningOnly since $purge-history is a server extension not every conformant server implements.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will accumulate history and then be purged

assert.description: Create must return HTTP 201

operation.description: Read back the Patient to use as the round-tripped update body
Round-trip update the Patient to produce a second version

assert.description: Update must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Purge prior version history for the Patient (server extension operation)

assert.description: Server extension: expect a 2xx success status when $purge-history is supported

operation.description: Read the current version after purging prior history

assert.description: Server extension: current version must still be readable after purge
Server extension: current version must remain the latest versionId

operation.description: Attempt to vread the purged prior version

assert.description: Server extension: primary expectation is 404 Not Found for a purged version
Server extension: alternative expectation is 410 Gone for a purged version

operation.description: Teardown: delete the basic delete-lifecycle Patient (already deleted by the test, best effort)
Teardown: delete the search-exclusion Patient (already deleted by the test, best effort)
Teardown: delete the hard-delete Patient (already hard-deleted by the test if supported, best effort)
Teardown: delete the $purge-history Patient
